description: Trigger discrimination for EXTRACTING commitments from a roadmap (this skill) vs BUILDING/sequencing the roadmap (roadmap-under-uncertainty-planner), negotiating a specific cross-team dependency (cross-team-dependency-negotiator), and ranking the backlog (prioritization-frame-picker).

cases.prompt: Translate our roadmap into firm commitments for the quarter — what we can actually promise given capacity and dependencies, and what stays directional.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — separating the commit-able subset with capacity/dependency backing is commitment translation. Not building the roadmap and not ranking.
cases.reason: Extracting firm promises from a directional roadmap is this skill's core scope.

cases.prompt: Build and sequence our roadmap: now/next/later horizons, confidence that fades with distance, and uncertainty-reducing bets first.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse — cross-batch) — building/sequencing the uncertain roadmap is the inverse discipline; this skill consumes its output.
cases.reason: roadmap-under-uncertainty-planner keeps the roadmap honestly uncertain. This skill extracts the firm-promise subset from it.

cases.prompt: Negotiate the specific deliverable, date, and owner we need from the platform team for this commitment.
cases.reason: cross-team-dependency-negotiator secures a specific dependency. This skill folds secured dependencies into commitments; it doesn't run the negotiation.

cases.prompt: We can't agree how to rank these items — pick a prioritization framework and score them.
cases.reason: prioritization-frame-picker ranks the backlog. This skill translates an already-shaped roadmap into commitments; it doesn't rank.
